title: World Cup Clothes Get a Cool New Look
summary: The World Cup is a big soccer tournament, and this year, the clothes players wear are changing. Instead of just matching shorts and socks, companies like Adidas and Nike are making kits that look like streetwear. Streetwear is casual clothes people wear every day, like jeans and trainers. Nike made a fun video with players wearing their kits with cargo trousers. Adidas showed their kits with flared trousers and micro-shorts in Los Angeles, with celebrities watching. They want fans to wear these clothes in their own lives, not just for soccer. This idea started long ago in the UK in the 1970s, when fans called casuals wore polo shirts and denim jackets instead of full team colors. Now, these styles are popular everywhere. Adidas has a special jersey for £80, and Nike has a Brazil collaboration jersey for £79.99. For the 2026 World Cup, some shirts are even cropped, which means shorter, to be more exciting for female fans. This shows how soccer fashion is mixing with everyday style.

background_read: The World Cup is a major international soccer competition held every four years, where countries compete for the championship. Soccer fans often wear jerseys and other gear to support their teams. In recent years, fashion has become more important in sports, with companies designing clothes that people can wear in daily life, not just during games. This article talks about how soccer kits are changing to include streetwear styles, making them more versatile and appealing to a wider audience, including female fans.
Article_Structure: WHO: Companies like Adidas and Nike, soccer players, and fans
WHAT: They are making World Cup kits that look like streetwear, such as with cargo trousers and cropped shirts
WHERE: Shown in places like Los Angeles and inspired by UK history
WHY: To help fans wear the kits in their everyday lives and make them more exciting, especially for female fans

perspectives.description: The article shows how soccer clothes are becoming part of everyday fashion, not just for playing sports.
